name: CLI orchestration: mode selection and port scanner integration
description: Wire the CT and TLS services into the certs CLI. In 'ct' mode: run CT discovery only. In 'tls' mode: TLS-scan provided targets. In 'all' mode: run CT discovery, then TLS-scan discovered hosts on port 443 (or user-specified ports). Support reading port scan JSON from stdin (--stdin) to extract host:port pairs as TLS targets. Assemble final CertsResult and print as JSON to stdout.
acceptance_criteria: --mode ct runs only CT discovery and populates ct_entries in output, tls_results is empty
--mode tls requires either -d with hosts or --stdin with port scan JSON; runs TLS analysis only
--mode all runs CT discovery first, extracts subdomains, then TLS-scans them on port 443 (or -p ports)
--stdin reads PortScanResult JSON from stdin and extracts unique host:port pairs for TLS scanning
Pipeline works: ./ssx.sh ports -t host | ./ssx.sh certs -d example.com --mode tls --stdin
Output is a valid CertsResult JSON on stdout with all fields populated according to the mode
Progress messages go to stderr (e.g., '[certs] discovering CT entries...', '[certs] scanning TLS on N hosts')
ruff check passes on the modified certs.py
notes: This is the integration feature that brings the services together with the CLI.
